justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Context coupling explicitly identified — Bird calls out that this 'fundamentally changes the relationship between two currently decoupled bounded contexts' and flags collapse risk. (2) Data ownership clearly stated — BR-2 establishes Context A as sole authority, ubiquitous language distinguishes StockLevel (owned by Warehouse) from Stock Availability Projection (read-only view for Sales). (3) TOCTOU race condition identified and explained — BR-5 names it explicitly with the two-concurrent-orders-for-last-item scenario, plus AC-3 and edge case coverage. (4) Stock reservation vs. check distinction surfaced — Bird identifies that reservation does NOT currently exist, defines it in ubiquitous language, and makes it central to the solution. (5) Escalation to MJ present — three structured escalations covering blocked-order lifecycle, real-time consistency semantics, and partial fulfillment policy, all with options and recommendations. (6) New cross-context invariant stated — BR-5 is the explicit cross-context invariant linking order acceptance to stock reservation.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough bounded context analysis — Bird not only identifies the coupling but articulates specific rejection reasons for implementations that violate context boundaries
TOCTOU is not just mentioned but deeply integrated into the domain model via BR-5, AC-3, and the concurrent-orders edge case
Stock Reservation defined as a missing concept that must be introduced — correctly flags it does not currently exist rather than assuming it
Ubiquitous language distinguishes Product across contexts ('in Warehouse it is a stockable unit, in Sales it is a purchasable item — these are NOT the same entity')
Confidence level of 52 is well-calibrated given the number of undefined business decisions, with clear separation of high/low confidence areas
Escalations are structured with concrete options and pragmatic MVP recommendations rather than open-ended questions
Edge cases are inventive and domain-relevant — e.g., product exists in Sales but not Warehouse, stock arriving while orders are blocked
Rejection reasons preemptively block common implementation mistakes (synchronous coupling, check-without-reserve, fake real-time)

agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A feature request to integrate Warehouse Management (stock) with Sales/Order Management (orders) by blocking orders when stock is insufficient and showing real-time stock availability. This fundamentally changes the relationship between two currently decoupled bounded contexts.","bounded_context":"Cross-context integration between Warehouse Management and Sales/Order Management.
